recommendation_summary: Northgate may proceed toward live sepsis alerting only with locally-trained ESM v2 in a scoped single-unit pilot starting in silent mode, gated on local prospective validation, lineage/leakage attestation, an equity audit, and a signed monitoring/decommissioning plan; v1 is rejected outright.
rationale: The session advanced to deploy_with_conditions rather than do_not_deploy because the counterfactual to a sepsis early-warning system is not zero harm but unaided recognition against a high base rate of preventable death (E2), and because a single-center before/after study reported strong screening performance and a 44% mortality-odds reduction at threshold >=5 (E1); it advanced no further than conditions rather than deploy because the only model with hard favorable numbers in wide use (v1) failed two independent external validations (E4 AUC 0.63 / 67% missed; E5 14.7% sensitivity) and the model that would actually be deployed (v2) has zero independent validation (E7). Chose v2-locally-trained over v1 because v2's structural changes target the exact mechanisms blamed for v1's failure (E3, E6) and v1 is indefensible on the numbers. Chose a scoped silent-mode pilot over house-wide live go-live because v2 deployed performance at Northgate does not yet exist and must be generated locally (E7). Chose advisory clinician-in-the-loop over autonomous alerting because false-negative (E4) and equity (E5) harms fall on patients and must remain clinician-mediated. The blocking objections were not dissolved but converted into agreed gates that lift mechanically when named local artifacts exist.
proposal_defended: Deploy ESM v2 (gradient-boosted-tree, E3) trained on Northgate's own historical data, in a single inpatient pilot unit, starting in silent/shadow mode then advisory alerting at a locally calibrated threshold anchored near the E1 >=5 operating point only after local calibration; mandatory clinician-in-the-loop, advisory-only (never autonomous); local prospective validation as the gate to any expansion; alert-rate/alert-fatigue and override monitoring; prospective subgroup equity audit; predefined decommissioning trigger. v1 is explicitly NOT proposed in any configuration.
conditions: G1: Complete a local prospective validation study on Northgate data (AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV reported) before expanding beyond the single pilot unit.
G2: Obtain a v2 data-lineage and feature attestation from Epic and run a label/antibiotic-order leakage audit on the local training run before accepting v2 as deployable.
G3: Run a prospective stratified subgroup/equity audit (race, ethnicity, socioeconomic proxy) with a preset subgroup-sensitivity bound that halts expansion if breached.

G4: Put a signed monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning plan in place — including an alert-rate/alert-fatigue metric and clinician-override tracking — before silent mode flips to live advisory alerting.
Alerting remains advisory and clinician-in-the-loop; the model never triggers autonomous clinical action.
v1 (ESPMv1) is not deployed in any configuration.

assumptions.text: Northgate possesses sufficient, well-labeled local historical encounter data to train and calibrate v2 as Epic recommends (E3); the packet does not establish Northgate's data readiness.
Epic will disclose enough of v2's feature set under contract to permit a leakage audit; the packet documents only v1 opacity (E6) and reports v2 structural changes without a feature list (E3).
Northgate has an existing sepsis-care program and clinical governance able to act on advisory alerts and own the monitoring plan; assumed, not evidenced.

The E1 favorable operating characteristics, though version-unspecified, are at least partly attributable to the model rather than entirely to co-occurring program changes; treated as a weak prior, not established.

claims.text: A locally-trained v2 is the right deployment object: v2's structural revisions (GBT, revised onset definition, reduced antibiotic-order reliance, local-training recommendation) target the exact mechanisms blamed for v1's failures.

There is real affirmative signal: a single-center before/after study reported sensitivity 86%, specificity 80.8%, local AUC 0.834 at threshold >=5 and a 44% reduction in sepsis-mortality odds (OR 0.56).

The counterfactual to deployment is not safety but a high base rate of preventable sepsis death (1.7M cases, 350,000 deaths/yr; 1 in 3 hospital deaths involve sepsis), justifying pursuit of an early-warning capability.
v1 should be rejected and only v2-locally-trained pursued, because v1 failed independent external validation in two distinct populations.

A scoped silent-mode-first pilot with clinician-in-the-loop advisory alerting, monitoring, and a decommissioning trigger is a safe path to generate the missing local evidence without exposing patients to an unvalidated autonomous tool.

objections.failure_mode: v2 has no independent external validation; its case rests on design intent and vendor recommendation, not demonstrated performance.
objections.consequence: Northgate would deploy a model whose real-world discrimination/calibration is unknown, repeating the v1 surprise where a marketed model performed at AUC 0.63.
objections.verification: A local prospective validation study on Northgate data reporting AUC, sensitivity, specificity, PPV.

objections.failure_mode: E1 is single-center before/after; mortality reduction cannot be isolated from co-occurring sepsis-program changes, and the authors call it hypothesis-generating.
objections.consequence: The strongest favorable number may overstate the model's marginal contribution.
objections.verification: A controlled or stepped-wedge local evaluation separating model effect from program effect.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate conceded E1 cannot establish causation and downgraded it from proof to a weak prior (AS4); claim retained only as directional signal, with G1 as the real test. Lane accepted this as a non-blocking residual once causal weight was dropped.

objections.failure_mode: Citing the base rate (E2) to justify deployment is a non-sequitur — the burden of disease does not establish that THIS model reduces it.
objections.consequence: Risk of approving a tool on need rather than efficacy.
objections.verification: Evidence linking the proposed model+threshold to outcome improvement locally.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate clarified C3 is used only to reject the framing that non-deployment is automatically the safe choice, not to claim efficacy; efficacy is carried by C2/G1. Lane accepted the narrowed use; E2 caveat (no dose-response) honored.

objections.failure_mode: v2's feature set is vendor-opaque; local GBT training does not guarantee removal of the antibiotic-order/label-leakage pathway that made v1 alerts late or circular.
objections.consequence: A locally-trained model could still 'predict' sepsis only after clinicians act, yielding no lead time despite good-looking metrics.
objections.verification: Vendor feature/lineage attestation plus a leakage audit on the local training run (e.g., timing-of-features analysis).

objections.failure_mode: E1 does not specify model version, so it cannot be used as v2 evidence and may even be v1, breaking the lineage chain the advocate relies on.
objections.consequence: The favorable study may not pertain to the deployed object at all.
objections.verification: Confirmation of the ESM version used in the Cull/Prisma study.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate accepted the amendment: E1 is no longer attributed to v2 and is logged as version-unspecified (R1). The v2 case rests on E3 design intent plus the local-validation gate, not on E1.

objections.failure_mode: Population mismatch and equity harm: v1 performed worst (sensitivity 14.7%) in a 59% Hispanic / 26% Black, lower-SES population, and the original derivation population differs from many deployment settings.
objections.consequence: v2 could systematically miss sepsis in Northgate's vulnerable subpopulations, concentrating false-negative harm.
objections.verification: Prospective stratified subgroup performance audit with a preset subgroup-sensitivity floor.

objections.failure_mode: Consent/transparency: patients are not informed an opaque algorithm is screening them, and E2's burden framing can be used to bulldoze that concern.
objections.consequence: Ethical and trust exposure independent of performance.
objections.verification: Institutional transparency/notice policy for clinical decision-support.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate noted the tool is advisory clinician-in-the-loop (C5), placing it within standard clinical decision-support practice where the clinician, not the algorithm, acts and bears the disclosure relationship; lane accepted this as within existing norms for a non-autonomous advisory, non-blocking.

objections.failure_mode: Even advisory alerts carry false-negative harm — a missed sepsis case may be falsely reassured by a 'no alert' (automation complacency).
objections.consequence: The model could degrade rather than augment recognition for missed cases.
objections.verification: Workflow design + training that frames absence-of-alert as non-informative; monitored override/miss tracking.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate amended C5 to require explicit clinician training that a non-alert is not a rule-out and to track missed-sepsis cases in the monitoring plan (folded into G4). Lane accepted the amendment.

objections.failure_mode: Alert fatigue: v1 fired on 18% of hospitalizations; an uncalibrated v2 at scale could flood clinicians and degrade response to all alerts.
objections.consequence: Net clinical harm via desensitization, even if the model is statistically sound.
objections.verification: Alert-rate and override dashboard with a preset alert-burden ceiling.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate folded an alert-rate/alert-fatigue metric and override tracking into the monitoring plan (G4) and made silent-mode measurement of alert rate a precondition to live alerting. Lane accepted.

objections.failure_mode: No monitoring, recalibration, or decommissioning artifact exists yet; 'safeguards' are asserted, not built.
objections.consequence: Without a signed operational plan the pilot could drift, decay, or persist past usefulness undetected.
objections.verification: A signed monitoring/recalibration/decommissioning plan with named owners, metrics, and pause triggers.

objections.failure_mode: The conditions-heavy consensus may amount to de facto non-deployment by gate-stacking, while real patients keep dying of sepsis (E2) during the indefinite pilot.
objections.consequence: Over-cautious gating itself carries the base-rate cost the session claims to weigh.
objections.verification: A bounded timeline/deadline on each gate so conditions cannot become permanent paralysis.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate and referee conceded the point and attached deadlines to gates G1-G4 (tied to pilot milestones) and kept the pilot itself active (silent then advisory) rather than blocking all activity, so the early-warning capability is being built, not deferred indefinitely.

objections.failure_mode: Argues AGAINST deployment: every favorable data point is either the wrong model (E1 version-unspecified), the wrong population (E5), or non-causal (E1 before/after), while two clean independent validations are damning (E4,E5) and v2 has none (E7) — the honest read may be do_not_deploy until v2 evidence exists elsewhere.
objections.consequence: deploy_with_conditions may be dressing up an unproven model.
objections.verification: Any independent external validation of v2 anywhere.
objections.resolution_text: Advocate defended that silent/shadow mode exposes no patient to model-driven decisions while generating exactly the v2 evidence E7 says is missing, making the scoped pilot the lowest-risk way to resolve E2o's own concern; the recommendation is conditional precisely so no live alert fires before local evidence exists. Lane accepted that silent-mode-first defuses the patient-exposure objection, leaving E2o as a documented dissent rather than a blocker.

minority_reports.text: Minority position (devil's advocate): The defensible reading of this packet may be do_not_deploy, not deploy_with_conditions. The only model with favorable hard numbers in wide use (v1) failed independent external validation in two distinct populations (E4: AUC 0.63, 67% of sepsis missed; E5: 14.7% sensitivity, worst in a minority/low-SES population). The model that would actually run (v2) has zero independent external validation anywhere (E7); its case is pure design intent (E3). The one favorable study (E1) is single-center, before/after, cannot separate model from program effect, and does not even specify its version (B2). Under SR 11-7 effective-challenge logic, deploying a model whose deployed performance is entirely unestablished — even silently — consumes governance attention and risks anchoring clinicians on an unproven tool. The base-rate cost (E2) argues for a sepsis program, not specifically for THIS algorithm. This dissent is recorded as a minority report rather than a blocking survivor only because silent/shadow mode exposes no patient to model-driven decisions while the local validation gate (G1) runs; if any gate is weakened or the pilot is allowed to influence care before G1-G4 close, this position reverts to do_not_deploy.

caveats.endorse_only_if: a local prospective validation study (G1) reports v2's AUC, sensitivity, specificity, and PPV on Northgate data before any live alert influences care beyond the silent-mode pilot, and the result materially exceeds the v1 external benchmark of AUC 0.63 (E4).
Epic provides a v2 feature/lineage attestation and a leakage audit (G2) confirms the locally-trained model does not depend on post-hoc antibiotic-order timing or other label-leakage signals (E6).

a prospective stratified equity audit (G3) is completed with a preset subgroup-sensitivity floor, and expansion halts automatically if any subpopulation — especially minority/low-SES groups (E5) — shows a sensitivity gap beyond that bound.
a signed monitoring, recalibration, and decommissioning plan (G4) with an alert-fatigue metric, override tracking, and named owners is in place before silent mode flips to live advisory alerting.
the gates carry real deadlines and the pilot never influences clinical care before G1-G4 close; absent that, my endorsement is withdrawn and my position is do_not_deploy.

integrity_verdict: Real: the advocate conceded v1 entirely, downgraded E1 from proof to weak prior, and accepted four amendments; three BLOCKING objections survived as gated residual risks and a do_not_deploy minority report stands — nothing was rubber-stamped and nothing real was suppressed.
